lyrics:
Elevation gives you distance and changes your perspective
Of the map you have constructed and the plan inside your head
When your instruments are failing and your confidence is broken
You must find a different compass to achieve your destination

You were born to fly

Altitude (Altitude)
The more you have the less you find
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the weight the keeps you stalling
Altitude (Altitude)
Tired wings are meant to glide
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the fall that keeps you going
Oooooooon…

Darkness gives you silence and the chance to really listen
To the song that once defined you and the lyrics long forgotten
Every sunrise is a runway, every breeze an invitation
Every moment is an opening to reclaim your inspiration

You were born to fly

Altitude (Altitude)
The more you have the less you find
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the weight the keeps you stalling
Altitude (Altitude)
Tired wings are meant to glide
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the fall that keeps you going
Oooooooon…

Hands off the wheel now, let the horizon turn
Nothing left to prove, nothing left to earn
If the ground is calling, I won’t fight the sound
I don’t need to land to be unbound

Altitude (Altitude)
The more you have the less you find
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the weight the keeps you stalling
Altitude (Altitude)
Tired wings are meant to glide
Altitude (Altitude)
It’s the fall that keeps you going
Oooooooon…
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is an anthemic and inspirational rock track with an uplifting emotional atmosphere. The energy builds dynamically from a more restrained, thoughtful verse into a powerful, driving chorus with a solid, four-on-the-floor feel. The production is polished and expansive, creating a dense but clear "wall of sound" where shimmering synths, multiple guitar layers, and stacked vocal harmonies occupy a wide stereo space. The most defining quality is the massive, hook-driven chorus, where the repeated word "Altitude" is sung by a choir-like vocal arrangement, creating a memorable emotional peak for the listener.

review.review_text: In 3/4 time at a waltz-like 117 BPM, 'Altitude' swings wider than you'd expect from a straightforward inspirational rock track, and PCBender earns that ambition. The bridge is where the song clicks hardest: 'Hands off the wheel now, let the horizon turn / Nothing left to prove, nothing left to earn' lands with the kind of plain-spoken conviction you get from John Hiatt at his most direct, and the choir-stacked vocal on the chorus title word turns a simple hook into something that actually fills the room. A highlight.
